arguments.command: find src -maxdepth 3 -type f | sort && rg -n "fib|Fibonacci|fibonacci|scroll|overflow|font" src

content.thinking:
**Adjusting number display**

I'm considering how to display large numbers without causing horizontal scrolling. It seems the page might be set to 38rem wide, leading to these issues. If I add a class like "fib-value" with CSS properties such as overflow-wrap: anywhere and word-break: break-all, it could help wrap large numbers nicely. Since the request is just about displaying these big numbers, I think I can modify the Fibonacci page to incorporate this style effectively.

arguments.newText: fib({n}) = {fib(n).toString()}

Updated `src/pages/fib/[n].astro` so long Fibonacci values wrap within the page instead of requiring horizontal scrolling.

Verified with `pnpm build`.
how `break-word` will work with large number?

`word-break: break-word` lets the browser split a long “word” when it would otherwise overflow.

A Fibonacci number is rendered as one continuous string of digits, e.g.

```txt
354224848179261915075...
```

Since there are no spaces, the browser normally treats it as one unbreakable word. With:

```css
overflow-wrap: anywhere;
word-break: break-word;
```

the browser is allowed to insert visual line breaks between any digits as needed:

```txt
354224848179261915075
764...
```

It does **not** change the actual number or insert characters into the HTML; it only wraps the displayed text so it fits the container without horizontal scrolling.
and how this changes will make it work?

The change targets the paragraph that contains the Fibonacci result: ```astro

fib({n}) = {fib(n).toString()}

``` and adds: ```css .fib-result { overflow-wrap: anywhere; word-break: break-word; } ``` Why that fixes it: - `fib(n).toString()` produces one very long uninterrupted string of digits. - Browsers normally only wrap text at natural break points like spaces. - A huge number has no spaces, so it can extend past the page width and cause horizontal scrolling. - `overflow-wrap: anywhere` tells the browser it may wrap that long digit string at any point if needed. - `word-break: break-word` adds fallback/compatibility behavior for breaking long words. So instead of this: ```txt fib(1000) = 434665576869374564356885276750406258025646... ``` forcing the page wider, it can display like: ```txt fib(1000) = 434665576869374564356885276750406258025646 436885276750406258025646605173717804024817 290895365554179490518904038798400792551692 ... ``` The number is the same; only the visual layout wraps it across multiple lines.
